close_reason: Implemented OutputEmitter interface, NDJSON emitter, refactored llmReason to streamText, added emitter support to toolDispatch. 49 harness tests passing, all quality gates green.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-n0l.4.md

Implement streaming of Output union events (text, code, tool_result, thinking, error from packages/contracts/src/output.ts) from the harness graph to the HTTP response during execution. Design: create an OutputEmitter interface that the graph nodes call to emit events as they occur. The emitter writes newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON) to the response stream. Each event is a JSON line matching OutputSchema. The LLM reasoning node emits text/thinking chunks as they stream from the model. The tool dispatch node emits tool_result or error after execution. Graph lifecycle emits error on failures. This replaces the current raw text chunk streaming in /v1/chat/stream. Client receives a stream of typed, parseable events rather than raw text.

Depends on: agent-platform-40r (ReAct loop wiring)
Parent epic: agent-platform-n0l (Agent Runtime Loop)
title: Structured streaming output

close_reason: Runtime config API merged (PR #32). Settings CRUD, dynamic rate limiter, full SonarCloud quality fixes.
description: Add a /v1/config or /v1/settings API endpoint to allow runtime configuration of rate limits (windowMs, max requests) and global cost budgets without requiring a server restart. Currently rate limits are env-var-only (RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS, RATE_LIMIT_MAX) and cost limits are per-agent via ExecutionLimits. This task should: (1) Create a settings endpoint for reading/updating rate limit and global cost budget config, (2) Store settings in SQLite, (3) Hot-reload rate limiter config without restart, (4) Add frontend UI integration when frontend phase begins. Discovered from: agent-platform-nqn.
title: Runtime configuration API for rate limits and cost budgets

close_reason: ReAct loop wired with mode router, stepCount enforcement, loop detection (3 consecutive identical calls), 6 tests passing
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-n0l.3.md

Wire the LLM reasoning node and tool dispatch node into a conditional ReAct loop in the LangGraph state graph. Flow: LLM node → conditional edge: if tool-call intent → tool dispatch node → back to LLM node; if text response (no tool calls) → END. Enforce maxSteps across the full loop (each LLM call + tool dispatch counts as one step). Add loop detection: if the same tool is called with identical args N times consecutively, halt with an error trace event. Update trace events to cover the ReAct cycle (llm_call, tool_dispatch, loop_detected). The existing plan-execute path should remain available as an alternative mode.

**Code review 2026-04-15 additions:** Design the graph mode selector in this task even though the plan path does not connect until agent-platform-dtc. Use PLANNER_GRAPH_INTEGRATION_ENABLED flag to gate the plan path. Entry: mode_select → [ReAct path] llm_reason → route → tool_dispatch → loop; [Plan path] plan_generate (stub/noop behind flag) → resolve_plan → execute. This prevents a second graph restructuring when the planner integrates.

Depends on: agent-platform-6d5 (Tool dispatch node)
Parent epic: agent-platform-n0l (Agent Runtime Loop)
title: ReAct loop wiring in harness graph

close_reason: Implemented session-aware POST /v1/chat with NDJSON streaming, legacy endpoint retained with deprecation header. 4 integration tests passing.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-n0l.5.md

Replace the current /v1/chat/stream endpoint (raw OpenAI pass-through) with an agent-aware endpoint. New flow: POST /v1/chat receives { sessionId, message } → load session from DB → load agent via Agent Factory → build AgentContext (tools, system prompt, plugins) → run harness graph with conversation history → stream structured Output events back via NDJSON. The endpoint must: resolve the agent's model config (or use default), pass conversation history through graph state, handle factory/graph errors gracefully with error Output events, clean up MCP sessions on request completion. Move or deprecate the old chat/stream endpoint. Add integration test: create agent + session via CRUD → send chat message → verify structured stream response.

Depends on: agent-platform-16f (Structured streaming output)
Parent epic: agent-platform-n0l (Agent Runtime Loop)
title: Session-aware chat endpoint

description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-n0l.2.md

Add a tool dispatch graph node to packages/harness. Receives LLM tool-call intent (tool name + arguments) from the reasoning node. Validates tool ID against agent allowlists using isToolExecutionAllowed. Resolves tool to either: (a) MCP session tool via callToolAsOutput using the MCP session from AgentContext, or (b) registry/native tool via a pluggable executor interface. Returns Output union (tool_result or error) to graph state so the next LLM turn can observe the result. Must handle: tool not found, tool not allowed, execution timeout, MCP call failure.

close_reason: LLM reasoning node with types, state fields, tool mapping, and 9 tests
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-n0l.1.md

Add a new graph node to packages/harness that invokes the LLM via model-router. The node receives: conversation messages (system prompt + history + user message), available tool definitions (from AgentContext tool manifest), and agent model config. It calls streamText/generateText and produces either a text response or one or more tool-call intents (tool name + arguments). Update HarnessState to carry messages, tool definitions, and LLM output. The node must not execute tools — it only produces intent.

**Code review 2026-04-15 additions:** When extending HarnessState, also pre-allocate empty fields for totalTokensUsed (number, default 0) and totalCostUnits (number, default 0). These are needed by task agent-platform-9yb (Execution limits enforcement) and adding them now avoids a second state migration later. Design the LlmOutput union type carefully — all 9 downstream tasks depend on it.

Depends on: agent-platform-yvd (MCP session lifecycle — Epic 1 complete), agent-platform-oss (error middleware fix)
Parent epic: agent-platform-n0l (Agent Runtime Loop)
title: LLM reasoning node in harness graph

close_reason: Timeout via Promise.race, maxTokens enforcement in llmReason, trace events extended. 7 tests.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-qlp.2.md

Enforce all ExecutionLimits fields beyond maxSteps (which already works). Implementation: (1) timeoutMs — wrap graph execution in a deadline timer; if exceeded, set halted=true, emit limit_hit trace event with kind=timeout, stream an error Output event, clean up MCP sessions. (2) maxTokens — accumulate token usage from LLM responses (Vercel AI SDK provides usage metadata); check cumulative total after each LLM call; halt if exceeded. (3) maxCostUnits — if the model provider returns cost information, accumulate and check against limit; otherwise skip (optional field). Add new trace event kinds: timeout, max_tokens, max_cost. Add HarnessState fields: totalTokensUsed, totalCostUnits. Unit tests: verify each limit type halts execution correctly.

Depends on: agent-platform-k7m (Plugin dispatch — need the full loop running to enforce limits meaningfully)
Parent epic: agent-platform-qlp (Harness Hardening)
title: Execution limits enforcement (timeout, tokens, cost)

close_reason: Planner-graph integration complete: planGenerate node, plan_failed trace, mode router wiring, feature flag enabled. 7 tests.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-qlp.3.md

Connect the existing planner package to the harness graph as an optional execution mode. Add a plan_generate graph node: takes the user message + agent context, calls the LLM with a planning prompt that requests structured JSON plan output, feeds the response through parseLlmPlanJson for validation, uses runPlannerRepairLoop on failure (max 3 attempts). On success, the plan feeds into the existing resolve_plan → execute loop. Add a mode selector in the graph entry: if the request specifies planning mode (or the agent is configured for it), route through plan_generate first; otherwise use the ReAct loop directly. Wire behind the existing PLANNER_GRAPH_INTEGRATION_ENABLED feature flag (flip to true when this task completes). Unit tests: mock LLM plan output → verify plan validation → verify execution proceeds. Integration test: agent with planning mode → verify plan generated and executed.

Depends on: agent-platform-9yb (Limits enforcement)
Parent epic: agent-platform-qlp (Harness Hardening)
title: Planner-graph integration

description: Document the harness security guard patterns for contributors. Cover: injectionGuard, outputGuard, mcpTrustGuard, PathJail, bash guardrails, domain allowlist, tool rate limiting, totalToolCalls limit, file size limits, risk tiers. Include how to add new guards, how guards compose in the tool dispatch pipeline, and testing patterns. Target: docs/architecture/security-guards.md or similar.
title: Security architecture contributor guide

description: The Architecture ADR lists HITL as a production requirement. LangGraph's checkpointer supports state persistence, but no suspend/resume API exists yet. Implement: (1) A 'pause' mechanism where the harness can halt execution at a tool-call boundary and await human approval, (2) State serialization so paused executions survive server restarts, (3) A resume endpoint that continues execution from the saved checkpoint. Requires: all Epic 2 + 3 work complete. Reference: code review 2026-04-15, future feature #5.
title: HITL (Human-in-the-Loop) pause/resume for agent execution

acceptance_criteria:
- All /v1 endpoints visible and testable via Swagger UI at /api-docs
- Schemas accurately reflect real API behaviour (request + response)

close_reason: All 4 tasks complete: OpenAPI spec, Swagger UI, tool contracts, validation middleware. PR #34 open.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-oai.md

Integrate an OpenAPI 3.0+ interface into the Express API to enable interactive Swagger UI testing, clear schema visibility, standardised documentation, and reusable tool contracts for the agent runtime.

Key deliverables:
1. OpenAPI YAML spec under /contracts/openapi with operationId, request/response schemas, status codes for all /v1 endpoints
2. Swagger UI served at /api-docs with try-it-out execution
3. Tool contract alignment — map operationId → tool name, request schema → input contract, response schema → output contract
4. Runtime consumption — harness can load OpenAPI specs and parse operations into tool definitions
5. Schema validation — validate incoming requests and outgoing responses against OpenAPI schemas
6. Separation of concerns — OpenAPI defines external contracts only, no business logic leakage

Constraints:
- TypeScript-compatible tooling
- OpenAPI as single source of truth for API contracts
- No schema duplication across layers
- Compatible with agent runtime tool registration

Optional enhancements:
- Automated contract testing (Prism/Dredd)
- Mock server generation
- Zod-based schema validation
- Dynamic tool registration from OpenAPI at runtime
title: OpenAPI integration: Swagger UI, contract-driven tool definitions, and schema validation

description:

Built-in observability layer collecting LLM/tool/request metrics. onMetric plugin hook for forwarding. Default: structured JSON logging. Optional trace persistence to DB.
notes: Deferred to post-MVP. Current extensibility (MCP servers, skills, pino logging, trace events) is sufficient for single-user local-first MVP.
title: Observability layer with pluggable metrics forwarding

close_reason: Merged to main via PR #30. AsyncLocalStorage correlation IDs, auto-injected by logger, Express middleware.
description: createLogger() produces structured JSON but has no request-scoped correlation field (requestId, sessionId, runId). As the runtime loop adds concurrent operations, tracing a request through LLM calls, tool dispatches, and plugin hooks requires correlation. Implement: (1) Use Node.js AsyncLocalStorage to propagate a request-scoped ID, (2) Middleware sets correlationId at request entry, (3) All logger instances automatically include the ID. Should be done before or during agent-platform-k7m (plugin dispatch wiring) when observability becomes critical. Reference: code review 2026-04-15, future feature #1.
title: Request-scoped correlation IDs for structured logging

close_reason: Implemented resolveModelConfig with precedence chain, updated chatRouter, 10 tests passing
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-n0l.6.md

Extend packages/model-router to support a resolution chain: agent modelOverride (provider + model from AgentSchema) → environment default (AGENT_OPENAI_API_KEY + default model) → system fallback. Add a resolveModelConfig function that takes an Agent and returns { provider, model, apiKey }. The LLM reasoning node uses this instead of reading model from the request body. API key resolution continues to use the existing gated key logic but is now scoped per-agent (an agent could potentially reference a different key via its model config). Update tests to verify the precedence chain. This task can be developed in parallel with 2.3-2.4 as it modifies model-router independently.

Depends on: agent-platform-9v1 (LLM reasoning node — needs the node to exist to wire into)
Parent epic: agent-platform-n0l (Agent Runtime Loop)
title: Model override resolution chain

close_reason: All plugin hooks wired: onSessionStart, onPromptBuild, onToolCall, onTaskStart/End, onError. 10 tests passing.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-qlp.1.md

Wire the existing createPluginDispatcher into the runtime loop so plugin hooks fire during real execution. Integration points: onSessionStart called when a new session-aware chat request begins (after Agent Factory builds context), onToolCall called in the tool dispatch node after allowlist validation but before execution, onTaskStart/onTaskEnd called if plan-execute mode is active, onPromptBuild called before each LLM reasoning call with the current messages, onError called on any graph node failure. The plugin chain comes from AgentContext (built by Agent Factory using resolveEffectivePluginHooks). Ensure the observability plugin produces structured log events during a real chat request. Add integration test: agent with observability plugin → send chat → verify structured events emitted.

Depends on: agent-platform-5pe (Session-aware chat endpoint)
Parent epic: agent-platform-qlp (Harness Hardening)
title: Plugin dispatch integration into runtime loop

description: The Architecture ADR requires rate limiting in the harness. Currently no per-agent or global rate limits exist. Implement: (1) Per-agent request rate limiting (configurable max requests per time window), (2) Global cost budget with hard stop (aggregate maxCostUnits across sessions), (3) Token budget alerting (warn when approaching limits before hard halt). description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-oai-validate.md

Add Express middleware to validate incoming requests and outgoing responses against OpenAPI schemas. Fail fast on schema mismatch, missing required fields, or invalid types. Use a TypeScript-compatible validator (e.g. express-openapi-validator). Ensure validation does not duplicate service-layer logic.
title: Add request/response schema validation middleware

close_reason: Fixed: generic 500 message + structured server-side logging + tests
description: The 500 catch-all in errorMiddleware.ts returns err.message directly to the client. For unhandled errors (stack traces, SQL errors, MCP connection strings), this exposes internal system information. Replace with a generic message for production: res.status(500).json({ error: { code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', message: 'An unexpected error occurred' } }). Log the full error server-side via structured logger. Reference: code review 2026-04-15, finding #2.
title: Fix error middleware information leakage

close_reason: Replaced SSEClientTransport with StreamableHTTPClientTransport. PR #28 created.
description: packages/mcp-adapter/src/transport.ts uses SSEClientTransport from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/sse.js which is deprecated. Replace with StreamableHTTPClientTransport or the current recommended transport per MCP SDK changelog. Independent of planned epic work — can be done anytime. Reference: code review 2026-04-15, finding #6.
title: Replace deprecated SSEClientTransport in MCP adapter

close_reason: Conversation history complete: messages table, Drizzle migration, repository functions, contract schemas, chat endpoint wiring. 8 tests.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-qlp.4.md

Design and implement conversation history storage for multi-turn chat sessions. Add a messages table to packages/db/src/schema.ts: id (text PK), sessionId (FK to sessions, cascade delete), role (text: user/assistant/system/tool), content (text), toolCallId (text, optional — for tool result messages), createdAtMs (integer). Generate Drizzle migration. Add repository functions: appendMessage, listMessagesBySession (ordered by createdAtMs), deleteMessagesBySession. Update the session-aware chat endpoint to: load prior messages for the session before invoking the graph, append the user's new message before execution, append the assistant's response (and any tool call/result messages) after execution. The graph receives the full conversation history as its messages input. Add contract schema for the message shape in packages/contracts. Unit tests: message CRUD. Integration test: multi-turn conversation — send message, get response, send follow-up, verify history is threaded correctly.

Depends on: agent-platform-dtc (Planner integration — all core runtime work complete)
Parent epic: agent-platform-qlp (Harness Hardening)
title: Conversation history / message store

close_reason: McpSessionManager implemented with parallel open, getSession, isHealthy, reconnect, closeAll. Factory integrated. 117 tests green. CI workflow fixed.
description:
Spec: docs/tasks/agent-platform-nzq.3.md

Build MCP session pool/cache for the Agent Factory. Given an agent's allowedMcpServerIds: open sessions to each MCP server using openMcpSession, cache sessions for reuse across requests within the same agent context, handle connection failures gracefully (log error, exclude failed server's tools from manifest, continue with remaining), provide cleanup function to close all sessions on session end. Add health-check/reconnect logic for long-lived sessions.

Depends on: agent-platform-2zy (Agent Factory)
Parent epic: agent-platform-nzq (Agent Schema & Factory)
title: MCP session lifecycle management
